# Questions in Livestream 171

Live Stream #171: A Census of Every Conjecture

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=506" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">8:26</a> | Why is older scholarship treated cautiously while church fathers are still quoted? | He says both older scholarship and church fathers can be useful, but both must be tested rather than accepted just because they are old. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=1229" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">20:29</a> | Why is the three-deben weight in your book shaped like a rabbit, and are all deben weights animal-shaped? | No. He says weights came in many forms, and animal shapes helped identify calibrated weights as weights rather than ordinary stones. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=1501" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:01</a> | Could the Merneptah Stele be referring to Israelites in the Sinai? | He says no, because the poetic geography and campaign setting point to Israelites in Canaan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=1724" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:44</a> | Why would Pharaoh want Hebrew boys killed if he still needed labor? | He ties it to fear of a large population allying with Egypt's enemies, especially in the Hittite war context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=2107" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:07</a> | Are humans a dichotomy or a trichotomy? | He says the Bible leaves room for both readings and does not settle the issue as cleanly as some traditions want. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=2360" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">39:20</a> | What is the purpose of the second death in Revelation 20:12-15? | He says it is not annihilation, but final separation from life in Christ, while still involving resurrection to judgment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=2980" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:40</a> | Can you give background on Numbers 30 and vows? | He says the passage is about vows and household obligations, not simply controlling women, and the text gives different cases for fathers, husbands, widows, and divorced women. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=3803" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:23</a> | What is your opinion on modern-day miracle claims, and have you ever witnessed any? | He says he has not personally witnessed a miracle, believes miracles can happen, but approaches modern claims with reserved skepticism and discernment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=4390" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:10</a> | Is the historicity of the Fall a hill to die on? | Yes, but he says the biblical claim should be kept nuanced and not expanded into unsupported claims such as no cell death or no entropy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=4662" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:42</a> | What current excavations might turn up significant Exodus-related data? | He points especially to work at Tell el-Retaba/Pithom and says more work south of the old estuary could help locate Pi-hahiroth or Baal-zephon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=5214" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:54</a> | What is your take on the bridegroom of blood in Exodus 4? | He reads the episode in its immediate context of Moses, Zipporah, circumcision, and covenant obligation, while stressing the passage is brief and difficult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=5641" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:01</a> | Why do you not use the Diatessaron much? | He says he uses Kurt Aland's Synopsis of the Four Gospels instead because it places the Gospel parallels side by side and is more useful for his work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=6012" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:12</a> | Is the harrowing of hell biblical? | He says it depends on what is meant: Christ descending to Hades or Abraham's bosom is biblical, but Christ suffering in hell is not. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=6324" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:24</a> | Is Revelation 9's fifth and sixth trumpet about the rise of Islam? | He says probably not; he reads the passage as angelic judgment imagery rather than a reference to Islam. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=7443" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:03</a> | Is life beginning at conception faith-based or scientific? | He gives a scientific answer: life begins when the new genetic complement starts cell division and meets biological criteria for life. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=7599" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:39</a> | Would Jesus have been able to cast the first stone at the adulterous woman? | He says no, because Jesus was not a witness; he also notes the John 8 story is textually disputed and the procedure in the story is irregular. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=7866" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:06</a> | What did Jesus write in the sand? | He says we do not know, and the story itself is not in the earliest manuscripts of John. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=8229" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:09</a> | Did Jews take more than one wife in Jesus' time? | Yes. He says some did, though economics limited the practice and it was not necessarily normal or desirable. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=8529" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:09</a> | What were the practicalities of chariots versus riding horseback, and did New Kingdom Egyptians ride horseback in war? | He says New Kingdom Egyptians used horses for chariots, not cavalry, because Egyptian horses were suited to pulling but not bareback riding. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=8778" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:18</a> | Why weren't women priests in ancient Israel, and does this tie to Egypt limiting female priests? | He says Egyptian priesthood varied by cult, while Israelite priesthood was limited to a very specific male Aaronide lineage and not simply to men in general. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=9163" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:32:43</a> | Did the English use Deuteronomy 22:5 correctly against Joan of Arc? | No. He says the English misapplied ritual law and treated armor as clothing for political rather than theological reasons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=9446" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:26</a> | Is sudden infant death syndrome the work of God or Satan? | He says he is not a physician and does not know the medical cause, but treats it as a consequence of the Fall rather than directly God's or Satan's work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=9622" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:40:22</a> | What do you think of the idea that the soul is not real and neuroscience or materialism will explain consciousness? | He does not find strict materialism compelling, saying that not fully understanding the soul or consciousness does not make them unreal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=9967" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:07</a> | After Jesus frees people from Abraham's bosom, where do righteous people go when they die? | He says that after the crucifixion, the righteous in Christ go directly to heaven rather than to Abraham's bosom. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=10324" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:04</a> | What happened to Prince Zannanza? | He says Zannanza was the Hittite prince sent to marry Ankhesenamun and was intercepted and killed in Canaan, likely by forces under Horemheb. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=10594" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:34</a> | If the cunning serpent is an angelic being, why is it described by God as a natural serpent in Genesis 3:14-15? | He says the passage uses metaphor and unlike comparison; the serpent language does not require a merely natural snake. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=11343" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:03</a> | Did Adam and Eve not fully understand God's rule because of their lack of knowledge? | He says they did understand the command not to eat, but Eve added "touch" to the command, which shows the danger of going beyond God's word. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=11618" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:13:38</a> | Who are your favorite sci-fi or fantasy writers with good technological realism, and how is your own writing coming? | He mentions Clifford Simak, Frank Herbert, Paul Anderson, and some Isaac Asimov, and says he has two completed sci-fi novels with another still developing. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=13000" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:36:40</a> | Why was pork hated or prohibited for Israelites to consume? | He says the exact Israelite reason is not fully known, but connects it to Egyptian influence and Israelite clean/unclean categories of normal and abnormal animals. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=13156" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:39:16</a> | Were tarot cards originally Bible cards used to teach people? | He says the older major cards were tied to biblical-story presentation before later divinatory reuse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=13393" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:43:13</a> | Is the word "blood" in Genesis 4:10 singular or plural in the original Hebrew? | He says it is plural, and explains that Hebrew can use plural blood language for shed blood on the ground. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=14187" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:56:27</a> | What would you recommend reading on Genesis 1-11 as temple ritual or sacred-space theology? | He recommends starting with John Walton, while saying the topic still has room for more work. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=14584" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:03:04</a> | How long has the manger scene been inaccurate? | He says the Magi-at-the-manger conflation goes back very early in Christian tradition, while carved display scenes are much more recent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=14742" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:05:42</a> | How did rich Roman aristocratic polytheists accept Jesus, and did Roman women accept Jesus more than Roman men? | He says many aristocrats resisted Christianity for economic and guild-related reasons, while women and slaves had fewer ties to those systems and often accepted it more readily. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/IArgOM3IUb4?t=15355" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:15:55</a> | What is the correct way to use "eh"? | He describes "eh" as a Canadian filler word, tag, and language softener, usually placed at the end of a sentence but sometimes used during pauses. |
